text: Learning to cook can feel overwhelming at first.
There are so many recipes, techniques, and ingredients to learn about.
The key is to start simple.
Pick a dish you already enjoy eating and try to make it at home.
Do not worry if the outcome is not perfect — even experienced cooks make mistakes.
The crucial thing is to keep trying.
Gradually, you will develop an instinct for flavours and textures.
One of the most rewarding aspects of cooking is the chance to experiment.
Substitute one ingredient for another and see what happens.
Add a herb you have never used before.
Each attempt enhances your skills and builds your confidence.
Cooking also provides a wonderful way to interact with family and friends.
Preparing a meal together can transform an ordinary evening into something special.
With patience and a keen appetite for learning, anyone can become a competent home cook.
